mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and residential housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a GDPC-only baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or forward returns turn negative when HOUST declines despite GDPC growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers market repricing of tighter future monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of periods following CPI spikes above 0.4% MoM, or the negative return signal disappears when FEDFUNDS remains near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes relative to SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between expansion and contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with declining consumer credit balances indicate deleveraging households are simultaneously re-entering the workforce, signaling sustainable demand recovery that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of triggered windows, or RSAFS declines independently of PAYEMS growth without affecting equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points quarter-over-quarter, corporate earnings revisions turn negative, causing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances following GDP deceleration shocks, or the signal's predictive power is fully absorbed by CPIAUCSL trends.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a simultaneous drop in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals stagflationary dynamics that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline in fewer than 40% of instances where the triple signal activates, or forward returns do not differ significantly from baseline when FEDFUNDS remains flat.
